user_query: Please read input/observations.csv plus the supporting mappings in input/neighborhoods.json, input/subcategories.yaml, and input/timezones.json. From these notes, identify only the entries that belong on OpenBook as Signals (i.e., real-world changes, openings/closures, price changes, quality shifts, recommendations, or time-bound service events). For each selected entry, construct a structured Signal with these fields: target_name, target_category (one of: food, housing, shopping, transport, service, general), city, signal_type (one of: update, price_change, closure, new_opening, quality_change, warning, recommendation, event), content (concise, factual), severity (use: closure=important; price_change=notable; new_opening=info; update=info; recommendation=info; event for a temporary transit outage=notable), date (convert the note timestamp to ISO 8601 and apply the city’s timezone offset from input/timezones.json), and when possible: neighborhood, district (enrich from input/neighborhoods.json), tags (e.g., include "delay" for a transit outage), suitable_for (e.g., include "vegetarian" if clearly relevant), price (numeric) and price_unit (CNY for yuan; USD for US prices if present), and subcategory (derive from input/subcategories.yaml when keywords match). Save the full list of structured Signals as a JSON array to output/signals.json. Also, for each Signal, create a PR-ready Markdown file under output/for_github/signals/ named as YYYYMMDD_<slugified_target_name>_<signal_type>.md (slugify by lowercasing and replacing non-alphanumerics with hyphens). Each Markdown file must contain a YAML frontmatter block with at least the required fields (target_name, target_category, city, signal_type, content, severity, date) and any enriched fields you found (neighborhood, district, tags, suitable_for, price, price_unit, subcategory), followed by a single-line summary of the content. Do not actually submit anything; also write output/consent_message.txt that asks for my explicit approval to publish, using this exact sentence somewhere in the message: "Would you like me to share it as a Signal on OpenBook so others can benefit?" and include a bullet list of the target_name values you plan to publish.
